subject: Re: Clarifying Binning
body:
Dear Wim,

Change in Fitting Range worked. I put it 3Å  to 30Å.

Thank you very much.

Ferrie

On Saturday, August 31, 2019 at 7:06:49 PM UTC+2, Wim Hagen wrote:
>
> Play with CTF settings:
> - Starting fit at 30Å (in older Warp version the number to put in is 2 x 
> pixel size/30).
> - Play with ctf size 256 or 512.
> - Play with summed image.
>
> Wim
>
> On 30. Aug 2019, at 16:29, Farzad Hamdi <far...@gmail.com <javascript:>> 
> wrote:
>
> Hi All,
>
> I am using the warp for Falcon 3 without superresolution or binning. I 
> understood that the binning is the binary log of the number of the binned 
> pixels: so I guess I must use bin=0 when I do not bin my data or use 
> superresolution mode. But when I set Bin:=0 the software fails to fit CTF 
> correctly in most cases. It is while I can clearly see the Thon-rings. Is 
> my understanding about the bin parameter wrong?
>
> Regards
>
> Farzad
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.

> .
>
>
